title: phlegmariurus holstii
intro: Phlegmariurus holstii, also known as Holst's climbing spleenwort, is a species of plant in the lycopodiaceae family, characterized by its climbing habit and the presence of a distinctive, fleshy rhizome. Traditionally, Phlegmariurus holstii has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce fever and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems, and is also used to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Phlegmariurus holstii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
